content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 19 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 109r–110v | **Source Images:** DEU_019_page_001.jpg, DEU_019_page_002.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), p. 155 | **LXX Image:** DEU_019_vaticanus_page_001.jpg

---

**1** When the LORD your God cuts off the nations whose land the LORD your God is giving to you, and you dispossess them and dwell in their cities and in their houses,

**2** you shall set apart three cities for yourself in the midst of your land that the LORD your God is giving you to possess.

**3** You shall prepare for yourself the way, and divide into three parts the territory of your land that the LORD your God causes you to inherit, so that any manslayer may flee there.

**4** And this is the case of the manslayer who shall flee there and live: he who strikes his neighbor without knowledge, and who did not hate him from yesterday or the day before—

**5** such as he who goes into the forest with his neighbor to cut wood, and his hand swings with the axe to cut the tree, and the iron head slips from the wood and finds his neighbor, and he dies—he shall flee to one of these cities and live,

**6** lest the avenger of blood pursue after the manslayer, because his heart is hot, and overtake him because the way is long, and strike him fatally—yet he was not deserving of death, because he did not hate him from yesterday or the day before.

**7** Therefore I am commanding you, saying: Three cities you shall set apart for yourself.

**8** And if the LORD your God enlarges your territory, as he swore to your fathers, and gives you all the land that he promised to give to your fathers—

**9** when you keep all this commandment to do it, which I am commanding you today, to love the LORD your God and to walk in his ways all the days—then you shall add for yourself three more cities beyond these three,

**10** so that innocent blood may not be shed in the midst of your land that the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance, and blood-guilt come upon you.

**11** But if a man hates his neighbor and lies in wait for him and rises against him and strikes him fatally, and he dies, and he flees to one of these cities—

**12** then the elders of his city shall send and take him from there, and give him into the hand of the avenger of blood, and he shall die.

**13** Your eye shall not pity him; you shall purge the [guilt of] innocent blood from Yisra'el, and it will go well for you.

**14** You shall not move your neighbor's boundary marker, which the former generations have set, in your inheritance that you will inherit in the land that the LORD your God is giving you to possess.

**15** A single witness shall not arise against a man for any iniquity or for any sin, in any sin that he commits. By the mouth of two witnesses or by the mouth of three witnesses a matter shall be established.

**16** If a malicious witness rises against a man to testify against him [of] apostasy,

**17** then the two men who have the dispute shall stand before the LORD, before the priests and the judges who are in office in those days.

**18** And the judges shall inquire carefully, and behold—if the witness is a false witness and has testified falsely against his brother—

**19** then you shall do to him as he intended to do to his brother. So you shall purge the evil from your midst.

**20** And those who remain shall hear and shall fear, and shall not again do any such evil thing in your midst.

**21** And your eye shall not pity: life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**v.4 "from yesterday or the day before"** — The idiomatic Hebrew *mitmol shilshom* (lit. "from yesterday, from the third [day]") = "previously / beforehand." EMET renders literally to preserve the Hebraic idiom of time.

**v.6 "avenger of blood"** — Hebrew *go'el ha-dam*, the kinsman-redeemer acting in the blood-vengeance role. EMET preserves this compound rather than smoothing to "blood avenger."

**v.11 "lies in wait"** — Hebrew *yitspon lo*, lit. "hides/treasures [against] him." The verb *tsaphan* captures premeditated ambush; EMET renders literally.

**v.15 LXX comparison** — Vaticanus (p. 155) reads *epi stomatos duo martyron kai epi stomatos trion martyron* ("by the mouth of two witnesses and by the mouth of three witnesses"), matching MT exactly. No significant divergence noted.

**v.16 "apostasy"** — Hebrew *sarah*, often rendered "rebellion" or "wrongdoing." The term in this legal context connotes a deliberate turning away; EMET opts for "apostasy" to preserve the theological weight of the root.

**v.19 "intended"** — Hebrew *zamam*, from the root "to plan/devise." The lex talionis of witness law: the punishment mirrors the plotted harm. Vaticanus has *ebouleueto* ("he deliberated"), consistent with MT.

**v.21 LXX comparison** — The five-fold formula (life/eye/tooth/hand/foot) appears identically in Vaticanus col. right margin of p. 155. No textual additions or omissions relative to MT.

*— EMET*
